title: My uncle who repairs old radios
summary: Describe an uncle who fixes old radios in his small workshop, honouring patient, skilled hands and old machines.
topic: A family member who repairs old machines
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a family member or neighbour who repairs old things (radios, clocks, bicycles). Describe their workshop, what they do, and why they keep old machines alive. Use present simple, past simple for one memory, and 'because' to give a reason.
hintStarters: My uncle has a small workshop behind his house, and there he…
He never throws an old radio away because…
When I was a child, he once let me…

hintVocab.meaningVi: phụ tùng / linh kiện thay thế

sampleText: My uncle has a small workshop behind his house. The shelves are full of old radios, and the air smells of dust and warm metal. Every day after lunch, he sits at his table and opens up a broken radio with small tools. He looks at the wires for a long time and then changes one small part. He is very skilled, and his hands are slow but sure. People bring him old radios from all over the town because new shops cannot fix them anymore. He never throws an old radio away because he says each one has a voice. Some of the radios are sixty years old, but they still work after he repairs them. When I was a child, he once let me switch on a big brown radio. Music came out of it, and I clapped my hands. My uncle smiled and said the radio was older than my father. Today people buy new things and throw them away fast. My uncle keeps the old machines alive, and I think that is a quiet kind of art.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a small workshop behind his house
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một xưởng nhỏ phía sau nhà ông ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: smells of dust and warm metal
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: có mùi bụi và kim loại ấm
mở một cái đài bị hỏng ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: his hands are slow but sure
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đôi tay ông chậm rãi nhưng chắc chắn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: never throws an old radio away
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không bao giờ vứt một cái đài cũ đi
mỗi cái đều có một giọng nói

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he once let me switch on a big brown radio
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ông từng cho tôi bật một cái đài lớn màu nâu
giữ cho những chiếc máy cũ còn sống
một loại nghệ thuật thầm lặng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple (routine) vs past simple (one memory)
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn cho việc ông làm hằng ngày ('he sits', 'he looks', 'he changes') và quá khứ đơn cho một kỷ niệm ('he let me', 'music came out', 'I clapped').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for what he does every day ('he sits', 'he looks', 'he changes') and the past simple for one memory ('he let me', 'music came out', 'I clapped').

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' for a reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để nêu lý do: 'He never throws an old radio away because he says each one has a voice.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' to give a reason: 'He never throws an old radio away because he says each one has a voice.'

sampleTranslationVi: Chú tôi có một xưởng nhỏ phía sau nhà. Những cái kệ đầy những chiếc đài cũ, và không khí có mùi bụi và kim loại ấm. Mỗi ngày sau bữa trưa, ông ngồi ở bàn và mở một cái đài hỏng ra bằng những dụng cụ nhỏ. Ông nhìn các sợi dây thật lâu rồi thay một bộ phận nhỏ. Ông rất khéo, và đôi tay ông chậm rãi nhưng chắc chắn. Người ta mang đến cho ông những cái đài cũ từ khắp thị trấn vì các cửa hàng mới không sửa được chúng nữa. Ông không bao giờ vứt một cái đài cũ đi vì ông nói mỗi cái đều có một giọng nói. Một số chiếc đài đã sáu mươi tuổi, nhưng chúng vẫn chạy sau khi ông sửa. Khi tôi còn nhỏ, ông từng cho tôi bật một cái đài lớn màu nâu. Nhạc vang ra từ nó, và tôi vỗ tay. Chú tôi mỉm cười và nói cái đài còn già hơn cả bố tôi. Ngày nay người ta mua đồ mới và vứt chúng đi nhanh chóng. Chú tôi giữ cho những chiếc máy cũ còn sống, và tôi nghĩ đó là một loại nghệ thuật thầm lặng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes the workshop?
options: The shelves are full of old radios, and the air smells of dust and warm metal.
The shelves is full of old radios, and the air smell of dust and warm metal.
The shelves are full of old radios, and the air smells of dust and warm metal it.
The shelves are fully of old radios, and the air smells of dust and warmly metal.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'shelves are' (plural) and 'air smells' (singular -s) both agree correctly.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'He ___ up a broken radio with small tools.'
options: open
opens
opening
opened

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes his hands most naturally?
options: He is very skilled, and his hands is slow but sure.
He is very skillful, and his hands are slowly but surely.
He is very skilled, and his hands are slow but sure.
He very skilled is, and slow but sure his hands are it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'hands are' is plural agreement, and 'slow but sure' are correct adjectives.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'hands' singular or plural, and do you need adjectives or adverbs?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why people bring him radios?
options: People brings him old radios because new shops cannot fix them anymore.
People bring him old radios because of new shops cannot fix them anymore.
People bring him old radios because fix them anymore new shops cannot it.
People bring him old radios because new shops cannot fix them anymore.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'People bring' is plural (no -s), and 'because' has a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the plural subject and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states why he keeps old radios?
options: He never throws an old radio away because he says each one has a voice.
He never throw an old radio away because he say each one has a voice.
He never throws an old radio away because of he says each one has a voice.
He throws never an old radio away because each one a voice has he says.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'throws', 'says', and 'has' all agree with 'he'/'each one'; the reason clause is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb endings and the structure after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence contrasts the radios' age and them still working?
options: Some of the radios is sixty years old, but they still works after he repairs them.
Some of the radios are sixty years old, but they still work after he repairs them.
Some of the radios are sixty year old, but they still work after he repair them.
Some of the radios are sixty years old, but still after he repairs them work they.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'radios are' and 'they work' are plural; 'he repairs' is singular; 'but' shows contrast.
mcQuestions.hint: Match all the subjects to their verbs.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence tells the childhood memory in the past?
options: When I was a child, he once lets me switch on a big brown radio.
When I was a child, he once let me switched on a big brown radio.
When I was a child, he once let me switch on a big brown radio.
When I am a child, he once let me switch on a big brown radio.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'was' and 'let' are past; after 'let me' use the bare infinitive 'switch'.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep the past tense and use 'switch' after 'let me'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the moment the music played?
options: Music come out of it, and I clap my hands.
Music came out of it, and I clapping my hands.
Music came out of it, and my hands I clapped them.
Music came out of it, and I clapped my hands.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'came' and 'clapped' are both correct past simple verbs.
mcQuestions.hint: Both actions are finished and in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence reports what your uncle said, naturally?
options: My uncle smiled and said the radio was older than my father.
My uncle smiled and said the radio is older than my father.
My uncle smile and say the radio was older than my father.
My uncle smiled and said the radio was more old than my father.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'smiled', 'said', 'was', and the comparative 'older' are all correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep the past tense and use the correct comparative.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states the main idea most naturally?
options: My uncle keep the old machines alive, and I think that is a quiet kind of art.
My uncle keeps the old machines alive, and I think that is a quiet kind of art.
My uncle keeps the old machines alive, and I think that is a quietly kind of art.
My uncle keeps the old machines alive, and a quiet kind of art that is I think it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'keeps' agrees with 'my uncle', and 'quiet' is the correct adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending and the adjective.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why might it matter to keep an old machine working when a new one is cheap and easy to buy, and is there an old thing in your family that you would never throw away?
